title: NEPA Electroporation of Emiliania huxleyi Cells
author: Inbal Nussbaum
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p4zdqx6

steps.instruction: Collect exponentially growing E. huxleyi cells by centrifugation at 3000 g for 3 min, room temp.
Wash the cells once and resuspended with 384mM Sorbitol to a final concentration of ~108 cells/ml.
Mix 150 ul of cells with 1–10 mg of (linearized) plasmid and transfer to an electroporation cuvette with 0.2 cm gap.
Electroporate with optimal conditions – we used 7 poring pulses of 250V, followed by transfer 10 +/- transfer pulses.
After electroporation, transfer IMMIDIATELY into 4 mL of fresh media and incubate to allow recovery in nonselective medium in the growth room, low light for 16–20 h.
Apply selection and cross your fingers…
